comment.body:
Alma, I’m working on my HMM and the GMM I’m feeding into it has 3 states and 2 mixtures and I’m using full covariance. This is all on python using the train_hmm and decode_hmm library. 

Should I be using something else like Anaconda or do I have too little mixtures and states? I’m feeding in the data from GARCH, adf test, OU model, and hurst exponent to create this model on price action for some context.

comment.body:
run a sweep over plausible values (e.g. 2–5 states, 1–4 mixtures etc)
train on 80 % of your series.. you don't want overfitting, nor too lean (less multimodality captured)
comment.raw_body:
run a sweep over plausible values (e.g. 2–5 states, 1–4 mixtures etc)
train on 80 % of your series.. you don't want overfitting, nor too lean (less multimodality captured)
